claim: Lymphadenopathy is observed in knockin mouse lacking the SHP-2 MAPK pathway.

result.claim: Lymphadenopathy is observed in knockin mouse lacking the SHP-2 MAPK pathway.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper ID 26851674. This paper describes the generation of knockin mouse lines where SHP2 signals were disrupted. Specifically, the gp130F759/F759 mice, which are identified as SHP2 signal-deficient, displayed both splenomegaly and lymphadenopathy. This directly confirms that lymphadenopathy is observed in knockin mice lacking the SHP-2 MAPK pathway.

evidence.text: Knockin mouse lines were generated by replacing the mouse gp130 gene with human gp130 mutant cDNAs.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: Expression of patient-derived GNB1 variants in Cdkn2a-deficient mouse bone marrow resulted in myeloid or B cell malignancies.
evidence.source: Mutations in G protein beta subunits promote transformation and kinase inhibitor resistance

evidence.text: Mice lacking junctional adhesion molecule A (JAM-A, encoded by F11r) exhibit elevated colonic lymphocyte numbers.
evidence.source: Compromised intestinal epithelial barrier induces adaptive immune compensation that protects from colitis.

evidence.text: Activity-induced LTP at identified synapses between nociceptors and spinal neurons projecting to the periaqueductal grey (PAG) was completely abolished in SNS-PKG-I(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: The gp130F759/F759 mice displayed splenomegaly and lymphadenopathy.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: Myeloperoxidase increased in lesion areas of myeKlf2(-/-)Ldlr(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Myeloid-specific Krüppel-like factor 2 inactivation increases macrophage and neutrophil adhesion and promotes atherosclerosis.

evidence.text: Researchers generated nociceptor-specific knockout mice lacking PKG-I specifically in presynaptic terminals of nociceptors in the spinal cord.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: Homozygous mutant mice showed a loss of ON responses to light.
evidence.source: Specific deficit of the ON response in visual transmission by targeted disruption of the mGIuR6 gene

evidence.text: Slc23a1−/− mice died within a few minutes of birth with respiratory failure and intraparenchymal brain hemorrhage.
evidence.source: Ascorbic-acid transporter Slc23a1 is essential for vitamin C transport into the brain and for perinatal survival

evidence.text: Deregulation of the Hippo pathway leads to cancer.
evidence.source: Regulation of Hippo pathway transcription factor TEAD by p38 MAPK-induced cytoplasmic translocation

evidence.text: These mice developed lymphoblastic leukemia.
evidence.source: Antiapoptotic BCL-2 is required for maintenance of a model leukemia.

evidence.text: Loss of PKCζ in mice results in enhanced intestinal tumorigenesis.
evidence.source: Control of Nutrient Stress-Induced Metabolic Reprogramming by PKCζ in Tumorigenesis

evidence.text: Homozygous mutant mice lacked mGluR6 expression.
evidence.source: Specific deficit of the ON response in visual transmission by targeted disruption of the mGIuR6 gene

evidence.text: In an experimental mouse model of lung fibrosis, AMPK activity is lower in fibrotic regions.
evidence.source: Metformin reverses established lung fibrosis in a bleomycin model

evidence.text: Mice lacking VGLUT2 selectively in SF1 neurons have impaired induction in liver of mRNAs encoding the gluconeogenic enzymes PEPCK and G6Pase.
evidence.source: Synaptic glutamate release by ventromedial hypothalamic neurons is part of the neurocircuitry that prevents hypoglycemia.

evidence.text: PTEN deletion increased PI(3,4)P2 levels in a mouse model of prostate cancer.
evidence.source: PTEN Regulates PI(3,4)P2 Signaling Downstream of Class I PI3K

evidence.text: An elevated presence of neutrophils and macrophages was observed in lesion areas of myeKlf2(-/-)Ldlr(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Myeloid-specific Krüppel-like factor 2 inactivation increases macrophage and neutrophil adhesion and promotes atherosclerosis.

evidence.text: Mice lacking VGLUT2 selectively in SF1 neurons have impaired induction in liver of mRNAs encoding PGC-1alpha.
evidence.source: Synaptic glutamate release by ventromedial hypothalamic neurons is part of the neurocircuitry that prevents hypoglycemia.

evidence.text: Mutant LRRK2 causes locomotor deficits in vivo.
evidence.source: Increasing microtubule acetylation rescues axonal transport and locomotor deficits caused by LRRK2 Roc-COR domain mutations

evidence.text: The knockout mice were designated SNS-PKG-I(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity
